# 'repo sync' should preserve dirty changes even if
# the current branch is merged and deleted during sync.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-05-18T13:59:12Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

gs repo init

# create and submit a feature branch
git add feature1.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature1' feature1
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #'

# dirty the tracked file on the feature branch
cp $WORK/extra/feature1_dirty.txt feature1.txt

# merge the PR server side and sync
shamhub merge alice/example 1
gs repo sync
stderr 'feature1: #1 was merged'

# the merged branch should be deleted
! git rev-parse --verify feature1

# we should land on trunk and keep the dirty changes
git branch --show-current
stdout 'main'
cmp feature1.txt $WORK/extra/feature1_dirty.txt

-- repo/feature1.txt --
Contents of feature1

-- extra/feature1_dirty.txt --
Contents of feature1
with uncommitted dirty changes
